text:
- **[NMP #2865](https://github.com/pablof7z/nostr-multi-platform/issues/2865)** — Phase 0 subissue: bring `nmp-nip60` into workspace/CI, capability-gate the false `pay_invoice` stubs, demote the legacy `kind:17375` relay-tag authority. [...] Removed-behavior audit for PR #2866. The two headline concerns you flagged both clear: WalletConfig is NOT serde-derived (the kind:17375 content is a hand-built JSON [key,value] pair array; `relay` lives in event *tags*, not content), so `relays`→`legacy_relay_hint` is a pure Rust field rename with zero effect on serialized/persisted/wire shape — NIP-60 round-trip is unchanged. And the removed WalletBackend/PayResult/WalletError trait + re-exports have NO consumers anywhere in the checkout (only self-references and unrelated nmp-nwc `pay_invoice`), so nothing downstream breaks. Ownership.rs's 7 `exclusive:true` kind claims (17375/7375/7376/7374/10019/9321/38172) have no colliding claim in any other crate's ownership.rs, and the D26 protocol-crate test already listed nmp-nip60. Genuinely thin. Two low-confidence candidates below.

[
  {
    "file": "crates/nmp-nip60/src/nip60_wallet/nutzap_send.rs",
    "line": 84,
    "summary": "publish_nutzap_info still writes legacy_relay_hint into the kind:10019 relays field the diff's own docs declare non-authoritative",
    "failure_scenario": "Every doc comment touched by this PR (lib.rs, mint_announce.rs, wallet_event.rs, nip60_wallet.rs, and the legacy_relay_hint() accessor) newly and emphatically asserts the kind:17375 relay tags are legacy residue that 'must never' be used as relay-selection source of truth — the authoritative set is kind:10019/NIP-65. But publish_nutzap_info still populates the *published* kind:10019 NutZap-info `relays` straight from self.legacy_relay_hint. kind:10019 relays are the ONE place NIP-61 treats this data as authoritative: senders read the recipient's kind:10019 relays to know where to publish kind:9321 nutzaps. A wallet decoded via from_wallet_event from a kind:17375 that had no relay tags gets an empty legacy_relay_hint, so publish_nutzap_info emits a kind:10019 advertising an empty relay set, and inbound nutzaps become undeliverable — the user silently receives nothing. The behavior is unchanged from before the rename (pre-existing quirk, not a code regression), but the PR's new contract now directly contradicts this write path, so it's the highest-value thing to flag." [...]     "line": 84,
    "summary": "The handle field `legacy_relay_hint: Vec<String>` duplicates `WalletConfig.legacy_relay_hint`, which the handle already holds inside `config: Arc<Mutex<WalletConfig>>`. Both `create_new` (line 112) and `from_wallet_event` (line 130) copy the same vec into two places. Simpler: return it from `config.lock()` and delete the redundant field.",
    "failure_scenario": "Two copies of the same state kept in sync by hand can diverge: a future edit that mutates the wallet's relay hint through `config` won't touch the handle's private copy, so the accessor silently returns stale relays while `build_wallet_event` (which reads `config.legacy_relay_hint`) writes the fresh ones." [...]     "summary": "The same 'the kind:17375 relay tags are a legacy, non-authoritative hint; kind:10019/NIP-65 is the source of truth; nmp-wallet owns relay policy; see nip60-nip61-wallet-design.md' paragraph is restated ~7 times across this PR: lib.rs:22-27, mint_announce.rs:11-16, nip60_wallet.rs module+field+accessor, wallet_event.rs module+field, nutzap_send.rs:74-79. Simpler: state it once (e.g. on the accessor) and link the other sites to it.",
    "failure_scenario": "Seven near-identical prose blocks must be edited in lockstep whenever the relay-acquisition policy changes; in practice they drift, and a reader who trusts the copy that wasn't updated is misled. Doc duplication carries the same sync cost as duplicated code without the compiler to catch divergence." [...]     "line": 23,
    "summary": "Re-adding nmp-nip60 to workspace.members now compiles a mandatory ureq TLS HTTP client + 409-line synchronous Cashu mint client (src/cashu/client.rs) into every `cargo build`/`cargo test --workspace`/CI run, even though the PR's own docs redefine the crate scope to 'NIP mechanics only — event codecs, types, pure shape validation' with all networking in the not-yet-existent nmp-wallet. Sibling HTTP-using crates (nmp-nip57, nmp-nip05) gate ureq behind an optional `native` feature (`native = [\"dep:ureq\"]`); nmp-nip60 makes it unconditional. Cheaper: gate ureq + cashu::client behind an optional feature, or move client.rs to nmp-wallet, so builds that do not exercise minting skip ureq + rustls/ring compilation.", [...]     "summary": "Rename is skin-deep on the WRITE path: freshly-built kind:17375 events still emit `relay` tags from `legacy_relay_hint`. The design doc's Relay Acquisition section sanctions only PARSING legacy tags ('parse it only as a non-authoritative compatibility hint'), i.e. read-compat for old wallets — not emitting new ones. `WalletConfig::generate(mints, legacy_relay_hint)` + `build_wallet_event` keep producing the exact residue activation was supposed to stop creating. Deeper fix: new kind:17375 should carry no relay tags (nmp-wallet owns relay acquisition); the constructor should not take a relay-hint param at all.",
    "failure_scenario": "Every wallet created after this PR still bakes relay tags into its kind:17375. When nmp-wallet lands and treats kind:10019/NIP-65 as authoritative, these self-emitted 17375 tags become live legacy data forever, so the 'legacy' hint is perpetuated by new writes rather than being a finite decode-only compat surface — defeating the purpose of the rename." [...]     "summary": "publish_nutzap_info writes the non-authoritative legacy_relay_hint into the kind:10019 NutZap-info event, which the PR's own docs declare the authoritative relay source",
    "failure_scenario": "The PR renames the field to legacy_relay_hint and adds doc comments everywhere insisting it 'must never be treated as the relay-selection source of truth' — the authoritative set is the user's kind:10019 relay tags. But publish_nutzap_info still sets `relays: self.legacy_relay_hint.clone()` on the kind:10019 event it publishes. legacy_relay_hint is populated from the wallet's kind:17375 `relay` tags (from_wallet_event). So the legacy hint is copied verbatim INTO the authoritative kind:10019 surface. Two concrete failures: (a) if the old kind:17375 carried stale relay tags, they are re-minted as the authoritative kind:10019 relay set, so nutzap senders resolving the recipient's kind:10019 will publish kind:9321 nutzaps to relays the recipient no longer reads and the recipient never sees/redeems them; (b) in the going-forward common case where kind:17375 has no relay tags, legacy_relay_hint is empty, so the published kind:10019 has zero relay tags — degrading recipients to NIP-65 fallback and losing wallet-scoped relay hints entirely. The docstring acknowledges this as an intentional default, so it may be by-design, but it directly contradicts the PR's stated 'drop legacy 17375 relay-tag authority' goal and is the strongest correctness/semantics candidate in the diff." [...]     "summary": "publish_nutzap_info() seeds the AUTHORITATIVE kind:10019 NutZap-info relay tags from `self.legacy_relay_hint` (the renamed field), directly contradicting the doctrine this same PR adds — that the kind:17375 `relay` tags are a legacy, non-authoritative hint and must NEVER drive relay selection. NutZapInfo.relays flows into the kind:10019 event's `relay` tags (nutzap.rs:49 builds them), which senders treat as the source of truth for where to publish NutZaps.",
    "failure_scenario": "A wallet decoded from a kind:17375 event carrying stale/legacy `relay` tags calls publish_nutzap_info(); the emitted kind:10019 advertises those stale relays as the recipient's authoritative NutZap-receipt relays. Senders zap to relays the recipient no longer reads, so incoming NutZaps are lost — the exact outcome the PR's new 'kind:10019 is authoritative, legacy_relay_hint is not' rule was written to prevent. The rename made the field name honest but left the one publish path that turns the hint into truth." [...] Assistant: All seven verifiers are running. The eighth candidate (the same relay-policy paragraph restated ~7 times across the diff) I can verify directly from the diff in front of me: confirmed by inspection — it appears in `lib.rs`, `mint_announce.rs`, `nip60_wallet.rs` (module doc, field doc, accessor doc), `wallet_event.rs` (module doc, field doc), and `nutzap_send.rs`. I'll assemble the final review when the verdicts land. [...] VERDICT: PLAUSIBLE — candidate crates/nmp-nip60/src/wallet_event.rs:88 (build_wallet_event still emits legacy 17375 relay tags)

Factual claims are all confirmed:
- `build_wallet_event` still writes relay tags: lines 87-90 `let mut tags = Vec::new(); for relay in &config.legacy_relay_hint { tags.push(Tag::custom(TagKind::custom("relay"), [relay.as_str()])); }`
- `generate` still takes the param: line 40 `pub fn generate(mint_urls: Vec<String>, legacy_relay_hint: Vec<String>) -> Self` (only renamed from `relays`, behavior unchanged).
- The write path is live via `Nip60WalletHandle::create_new` (nip60_wallet.rs:96-101), which passes its `relays` arg straight into `generate` → emitted as tags on every brand-new wallet.
- Confirmed there are no non-test callers of `create_new`/`generate` in-repo (nmp-wallet doesn't exist yet), so the write path is currently dormant.

But the "contradicts PR goal" framing overreaches, so not CONFIRMED. The design doc's governing sentence targets READ authority only: "Activation should remove that as the source of truth, or parse it only as a non-authoritative compatibility hint that cannot override kind:10019 or NIP-65 fallback." It offers two options; the PR picked option 2 (parse as hint) and delivered on it — renamed the field, demoted it in docs everywhere, and nothing reads it as authoritative. The doc says nothing prohibiting *emission*. Decode→rebuild preserving tags is legitimate round-trip fidelity, and for new wallets the caller controls what relays get written. So this is a real design tension (new wallets don't age the legacy surface out) but a defensible judgment call rather than a defect against a stated requirement — PLAUSIBLE. [...] (a) Both copies exist and are populated in both constructors — CONFIRMED.
- Handle field: `nip60_wallet.rs:84  legacy_relay_hint: Vec<String>,`
- create_new: `nip60_wallet.rs:112  legacy_relay_hint: relays,` while config gets the same vec via `WalletConfig::generate(vec![mint_url...], relays.clone())` (line 101).
- from_wallet_event: `nip60_wallet.rs:130  let legacy_relay_hint = config.legacy_relay_hint.clone();` then `:136  legacy_relay_hint,`. Both places seeded from the identical source.

(b) Mutation path after construction — NONE EXISTS. The only `config.lock()` sites are `nutzap_send.rs:81` and `nutzap_receive.rs:28`, and both immediately `.clone()` for read-only use; neither mutates `config.legacy_relay_hint`. `build_wallet_event` is called only in `create_new` (construction) and one test — there is no update_wallet / event-rebuild flow. So divergence between the two copies is purely hypothetical today. Per your own criterion this pushes to PLAUSIBLE, not CONFIRMED.

(c) Zero callers of the accessor — CONFIRMED. `grep "legacy_relay_hint()"` returns no call sites anywhere in the workspace; every `Self::legacy_relay_hint` hit (lib.rs:26, mint_announce.rs:15, nutzap_send.rs:75) is a doc-comment rustdoc link, not a call. The two real reads use the field directly, not the accessor.

(d) The two divergent read sites — quoted:
- publish_nutzap_info reads the HANDLE copy: `nip60_wallet/nutzap_send.rs:84  relays: self.legacy_relay_hint.clone(),`
- build_wallet_event reads the CONFIG copy: `wallet_event.rs:88  for relay in &config.legacy_relay_hint {`

Summary: The redundant field and the two independent read sites are real, so the simplification (collapse to a single source, likely reading through `config`) is legitimate. But because no code path ever mutates `config.legacy_relay_hint` post-construction, the two copies cannot actually diverge today — the bug is latent, not live. The unused `legacy_relay_hint()` accessor (line 223) is genuinely dead code and safe to drop. Net: a valid cleanup, not a live bug → PLAUSIBLE. [...] VERDICT: REFUTED — crates/nmp-nip60/src/ownership.rs:85-95 (kind:38172 exclusive claim)

(a) TRUE that 38172/NIP-88 is absent from the doc: grep of docs/architecture/nip60-nip61-wallet-design.md for "38172|NIP-88|nip88" returns zero hits, and the "Existing NIP Crates" section (lines 71-72) lists only "kind:17375, kind:7375, kind:7376, kind:7374, kind:10019, and kind:9321."

(b) The claim reflects real, shipped code. crates/nmp-nip60/src/mint_announce.rs is a full kind:38172 codec: line 1 "//! NIP-88 mint announcement event (kind:38172)", a `MintAnnouncement` struct, `build_mint_announce_event`, a decoder, and a subscription filter (`.kind(Kind::from(KIND_MINT_ANNOUNCE as u16))`). The exclusive claim guards code that exists.

(c) Doc-basis is NOT the workspace norm — code-implements-it is. nmp-nip60 is the ONLY crate with a design doc enumerating its kind ownership (grep for "Existing NIP Crates|owns reusable" across docs/ returns just this doc + one unrelated content-rendering doc). Sampled peers claim exclusive kinds with NO design-doc backing at all: nmp-nip57/src/ownership.rs claims 9734 and 9735 (both `exclusive: true`), nmp-blossom/src/ownership.rs claims 24242 (`exclusive: true`), and neither crate even has an architecture design doc (grep of docs/architecture/ for these kinds = zero). So an exclusive linker-symbol claim on an implemented kind absent from any design doc is exactly the established convention, not a violation of it.

The candidate's premise — that a doc must sanction the kind for the exclusive claim to be legitimate — is contradicted by the majority of the workspace. This candidate does not describe a defect. [...] VERDICT: CONFIRMED — crates/nmp-nip60/src/ownership.rs:97-108

The `owns` text on the exclusive `mechanism` claim `nip60.cashu_backend_adapter` — "Cashu proof/DLEQ/P2PK/rollover types **and the Cashu backend adapter seam surface consumed by nmp-wallet**" — is inconsistent with both the code state and the design doc, and all three sub-issues verify:

(1) DELETED code: `git diff master...pr2866` shows `crates/nmp-nip60/src/backend.rs` removed (82 deletions: the `WalletBackend` trait, `PayResult`, `WalletError`), plus `pub mod backend;` and `pub use backend::{PayResult, WalletBackend, WalletError};` removed from lib.rs. `ls backend.rs` = No such file. So the "seam surface consumed by nmp-wallet" phrase describes code that no longer exists in this crate after the PR.

(2) adapter-vs-seam conflation: design doc is explicit — line 101 "`WalletBackend` lives in `nmp-wallet`, not `nmp-core` and not `nmp-nip60`"; line 75 (Existing NIP Crates) nmp-nip60 owns "a Cashu backend adapter **for** the `nmp-wallet::WalletBackend` seam." The doc cleanly separates *adapter* (nip60's) from *seam* (nmp-wallet's). The claim's phrase "the Cashu backend adapter **seam surface** consumed by nmp-wallet" fuses the two and reads as claiming the seam — which the crate's own note[0] (ownership.rs:112-115) then contradicts: "the unified WalletBackend seam ... are owned by nmp-wallet ... not this crate." Internal tension between the `owns` text and its own note.

(3) bundling: one exclusive id bundles settled crypto-type ownership (Cashu proof/DLEQ/P2PK/rollover — legitimate, matches doc line 73) with the contested/nonexistent "seam surface" wording in a single `owns` string.

CAVEAT on severity / enforcement: this is a documentation-accuracy finding, not a functional or linker bug. Per crates/nmp-ownership/src/macros.rs:15 the exclusive linker symbol is `concat!("__nmp_own__", claim_type, "__", scope_kind, "__", scope_value, "__", context)` = `__nmp_own__mechanism__namespace__nmp-nip60__`. It keys ONLY on claim_type + scope kind/value/context — NOT on the `id` and NOT on the `owns` text (lib.rs:59-63 confirms collision is scope-symbol based). So `exclusive: true` here merely reserves the (mechanism, namespace=nmp-nip60) scope against another crate; the "seam surface" wording is purely descriptive prose and causes no collision. The finding is real (the prose misdescribes deleted code and conflates adapter/seam against the doc), but its impact is claim-text accuracy, not runtime/build behavior. [...] 
(a) ureq IS unconditional in nmp-nip60/Cargo.toml. It has NO [features] section at all, and:
    `ureq = { version = "2", default-features = false, features = ["tls", "json"] }`  (no `optional = true`). CONFIRMED.

(b) Siblings DO gate ureq behind an optional `native` feature. CONFIRMED:
  nmp-nip57/Cargo.toml:
    `[features]` / `native = ["dep:ureq"]`
    `ureq = { version = "2", optional = true, default-features = false, features = ["tls", "json"] }`
  nmp-nip05/Cargo.toml:
    `native = ["dep:ureq"]`
    `ureq = { version = "2", optional = true, default-features = false, features = ["tls", "json"] }` [...] The PR does what issue #2865 asked: returns `nmp-nip60` to workspace/CI membership (dropping the standalone `[workspace]` stub and inheriting workspace package fields), deletes the dead `WalletBackend` trait whose `pay_invoice` always failed, renames the kind:17375 relay data to `legacy_relay_hint` with non-authoritative framing throughout, and adds the ownership declaration and release classification the gates require. Eight finder angles and seven adversarial verifiers later: the mechanical work is solid — no serde/wire breakage (the 17375 content is hand-built JSON, tags carry the relays), zero consumers of the deleted trait, no ownership collisions, and every doctrine/manifest/file-size gate passes. Six findings survived verification.

**Findings, most severe first:**

1. `crates/nmp-nip60/src/nip60_wallet/nutzap_send.rs:84` — **`publish_nutzap_info` seeds the *authoritative* kind:10019 relay tags from `legacy_relay_hint`, the exact value the PR's own docs say "must never" drive relay selection** (found independently by 5 angles; PLAUSIBLE — every hop confirmed, but the method currently has zero callers and `build_nutzap_info_event` is a public escape hatch). Failure mode if an app adopts it: a wallet decoded from a stale or relay-less 17375 publishes a 10019 advertising stale/empty relays, senders deliver kind:9321 nutzaps to relays the user never reads, and value is silently lost. The doc comment saying callers "should prefer" a resolved set describes a capability the signature doesn't offer. Suggested fix: give it a `relays: &[String]` parameter (or delete the helper until `nmp-wallet` owns 10019 publication).

2. `crates/nmp-nip60/src/ownership.rs:102` — **the exclusive mechanism claim's `owns` text says the crate owns "the Cashu backend adapter *seam surface* consumed by nmp-wallet" — but this same PR deletes all adapter code, and the design doc explicitly places the seam in `nmp-wallet`, "not nmp-nip60"** (CONFIRMED; doc-accuracy severity — the linker symbol keys only on scope, so no gate fails). The claim even contradicts its own `notes` entry four lines down. Suggested fix: reword to the settled part only ("Cashu proof/DLEQ/P2PK/rollover types; adapter *for* the nmp-wallet seam").

3. `crates/nmp-nip60/src/wallet_event.rs:88` — **the demotion is read-side only: `build_wallet_event` still emits `relay` tags on every newly built kind:17375, and `WalletConfig::generate` still takes the hint parameter** (PLAUSIBLE — the design doc's sentence technically governs read authority, and the path is dormant until a caller exists, but new wallets will keep minting the "legacy residue" instead of letting it age out). Worth deciding now whether new 17375s should carry relay tags at all.

4. `crates/nmp-nip60/src/nip60_wallet.rs:84` — **`legacy_relay_hint` is stored twice** (handle field + inside `config: Arc<Mutex<WalletConfig>>`), with the two live readers split across the copies (`publish_nutzap_info` reads the handle, `build_wallet_event` reads the config), and the renamed public accessor at line 223 has zero callers workspace-wide (PLAUSIBLE — divergence is latent since nothing mutates post-construction). Collapse to one source and drop the dead accessor.

5. `crates/nmp-nip60/Cargo.toml:34` — **`ureq` is unconditional while the crate's siblings (`nmp-nip57`, `nmp-nip05`) gate it behind `native = ["dep:ureq"]`** (PLAUSIBLE — as a consistency matter only; the build-cost argument was refuted since `nmp-blossom` and `nmp-nip11` already compile ureq+TLS unconditionally). Gating the mint client behind the same `native` feature would match convention and keep the codec surface HTTP-free for wasm-ish consumers.

6. Doc prose — the same "legacy, non-authoritative; kind:10019/NIP-65 authoritative; policy lives in nmp-wallet; see design doc" paragraph is restated roughly seven times across `lib.rs`, `mint_announce.rs`, `nip60_wallet.rs` (×3), `wallet_event.rs` (×2), and `nutzap_send.rs` (CONFIRMED by inspection). State it once on the accessor and link the rest — seven copies will drift.

**Verified non-issues worth knowing:** the field rename cannot break persisted wallets (relays live in tags, config JSON is hand-built key-pairs); the `nostr = "0.44"` literal pin is an explicit documented workspace policy boundary, not a missed migration; the kind:38172 exclusive claim without design-doc mention matches workspace norm (nmp-nip57/nmp-blossom do the same); and the release dry-run only runs `cargo package --list` over the new `[[public_crates]]` entry, with all path deps also public. [...] - **Commented on epic #2864** flagging the sequencing that matters: the kind:10019 relay-seeding fix (item 1) should land **before Phase 1 gives `publish_nutzap_info` its first caller** — it's dormant now, which is exactly why it's cheap to fix today and expensive to discover later.
